concerns.text: FR-003 (generate diverse novel objects) and SC-001/SC-002 (statistical comparison) lack a completed task producing the 'NovelObjectSet' artifacts. T007 (generator class) and T007a (execution) are marked `[ ]` (incomplete). Without a task explicitly generating the 30 novel geometries in `data/generated/`, the evaluation dataset is undefined, making the core zero-shot adaptation hypothesis untestable.

SC-003 (6h limit) and SC-004 (7GB RAM) have no verified implementing task. T029 (benchmark execution) is marked `[ ]` (incomplete). The plan requires measuring wall-clock time and peak memory against these constraints, but no task exists to run the full end-to-end benchmark and log these specific metrics to `data/results/`.

Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) requires a runnable project structure and pinned dependencies. T001 (project structure), T002 (requirements.txt), T003 (pytest.ini), and T009 (state YAML) are marked `[ ]` (incomplete). Without these foundational tasks, the project cannot be executed or verified, blocking all subsequent user stories.

Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) mandates checksumming of datasets. T009a (checksum verification logic) is marked `[ ]` (incomplete). There is no task producing the code or script to compute/store checksums for `data/raw` and `data/generated`, leaving the data hygiene requirement unimplemented.

FR-005 (paired t-test) and SC-001/SC-002 require a 'static PICA baseline' for comparison. T012c (baseline_runner.py) is marked `[X]` but the description does not explicitly verify that the baseline is loaded from a canonical source or that its parameters are fixed. This risks an unverifiable comparison, failing the success criteria.

T015 (integrate generation with evaluation) is marked `[X]` but contains `<!-- FAILED: unspecified -->` and depends on T007a (generation), which is incomplete. This breaks the traceability between FR-003 (generate objects) and FR-005 (paired t-test), as the evaluation loop has no defined input data source.
